test.notes: Test infrastructure not yet implemented - testing-coverage-infrastructure feature is pending in features.json

typecheck.notes: TypeScript compilation passed for all reviewed features

lint.notes: Lint script not configured in package.json - not yet implemented

nonBlockingIssues.description: Async cleanup race condition in workspaceStore - cleanup IPC call not awaited before workspace switch completes
Excessive console.log statements in TerminalCell cleanup - should use centralized Logger class instead
Blocking spawnSync in cleanupAllTerminals loop - may cause delay with many terminals, consider parallel execution

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add decision point for scenarios where test infrastructure is missing: (a) set up basic test infrastructure first as part of the feature, or (b) document manual verification steps required and create follow-up feature for automated tests
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All 4 features skipped writing automated tests because test infrastructure is not configured. Workers used TypeScript compilation and build verification as interim validation instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit convention requiring use of Logger class (src/lib/logger.ts) instead of direct console.log statements. Document that console.log should only be used for critical verification during memory leak debugging, and should be replaced with Logger.debug/info/warn in production code.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: critical-workspace-switch-cleanup feature added 7+ console.log statements in cleanup path. Codebase has centralized Logger class with proper levels and prefixes documented in CLAUDE.md, but convention not enforced.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Mark test and lint commands as 'not_yet_implemented' or remove them entirely to avoid confusion. Test infrastructure is a separate pending milestone (testing-infrastructure).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: services.yaml declares 'test: bun run test' and 'lint: bun run lint' but these scripts don't exist in package.json. Workers discovered this and used TypeScript compilation instead.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Already updated by workers with comprehensive documentation covering VAL-MEM-001 through VAL-MEM-004. Add xterm.js API quirk about attachCustomKeyEventHandler returning void (requires cleanup flag pattern).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: critical-xtermjs-event-disposal worker discovered this xterm.js API behavior. Memory-optimization.md was created with 431 lines of documentation covering disposal patterns, verification checklists, and troubleshooting.

salientSummary: All 4 critical-memory-leak-fixes features PASSED code review. Implementation addresses VAL-MEM-001 through VAL-MEM-004 with proper cleanup sequences, IPC listener disposal, xterm.js event handling, and Windows process tree killing. Validators: typecheck passed, test/lint not implemented yet (separate milestone). Three non-blocking issues identified: async cleanup race condition, excessive console.log usage, blocking spawnSync in loop. Key systemic gap: memory-leak-fixer skill needs guidance for scenarios without test infrastructure.

VAL-MEM-001.notes: Workspace switch cleanup implemented with proper PTY termination, xterm.js disposal, IPC listener removal, ResizeObserver disconnection

VAL-MEM-002.notes: TerminalCell useEffect cleanup disposes all resources with proper error handling and null checks

VAL-MEM-003.notes: IPC event listeners properly unsubscribed with verification logging, listenersRef.current cleared after cleanup

VAL-MEM-004.notes: xterm.js event handlers stored as IDisposable and properly disposed, cleanup flag pattern for attachCustomKeyEventHandler

VAL-FLOW-001.notes: Workspace switching stress test addressed through complete cleanup implementation

VAL-PERF-002.notes: Scrollback buffer configured to 1000 lines to prevent memory bloat

VAL-PERF-003.notes: Windows process tree killing implemented using taskkill /f /t with fallback logic

nextSteps: User-testing-validator will run automatically to validate assertions through real user surface
Address non-blocking issues in follow-up PR (async race condition, Logger adoption, parallel cleanup)
Complete testing-infrastructure milestone to enable automated validation
Update memory-leak-fixer skill with guidance for missing test infrastructure scenarios
